jokes.text: You know what? You better get a new girlfriend, all right? 'Cause this isn't gonna last that much longer.
jokes.setup: Larry offering relationship advice to his blind friend
jokes.punchline: Immediately predicting the relationship's doom with typical Larry bluntness
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David brutal honesty that creates immediate discomfort while being character-specific

jokes.text: Hey, I did you a favor. The problem is it's hard to fix you up. You're so particular.
jokes.setup: Michael blaming Larry for his lack of girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Larry defending himself by insulting Michael's standards
jokes.explanation: Good character work but fairly standard Larry defensive rationalization

jokes.text: Not only does she have to be good-looking, but she's going out with a blind man. She has to wait on hand and foot.
jokes.setup: Discussing the challenges of dating for a blind person
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical take that women would have to be servile to date a blind man
jokes.explanation: Dark but insightful observation that's quintessentially Larry's warped worldview

jokes.text: Okay, first of all, you've got nothing inside of you, okay? Let me just make that clear. You are the most superficial man I've ever met, blind or sighted, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry claiming women care about what's inside
jokes.punchline: Michael's devastating counter-attack about Larry's superficiality
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with the 'blind or sighted' qualifier being particularly sharp and quotable

jokes.text: Theater performance interrupted by yelling 'Argh! Argh!'
jokes.punchline: Performance dissolving into inarticulate yelling
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy moment, not particularly sophisticated

jokes.text: Oh, okay, yoghurt. Is that a prefix, you think, 'yo'? Yo-ga, yo-ghurt. Think that's related... -ghurt, -ga?
jokes.punchline: Larry's pseudo-linguistic analysis of the 'yo' prefix
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry overthinking a simple mishearing into elaborate theory

jokes.text: Hey, I thought of some more yos. Yodel, have you ever yodeled?
jokes.punchline: Continuing to find more 'yo' words obsessively
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke showing Larry's obsessive nature

jokes.text: I'm not gonna drive you around like a chauffeur. Get in the front seat.
jokes.setup: Michael sitting in the back seat
jokes.punchline: Larry's bizarre insecurity about appearing like a chauffeur
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David neurosis - creating conflict over imaginary social perception

jokes.text: What kind of person is so insecure that they have to make somebody move into the front seat so they don't think they're driving somebody around?
jokes.punchline: Michael's precise psychological analysis of Larry's insecurity
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that perfectly skewers Larry's ridiculous position

jokes.text: The kind of person that's so insecure that needs to be driven around. Subliminally you're telling me you need me to drive you around.
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely backwards psychological interpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic inversion - making himself the victim

jokes.text: You're such a baby. You're a grown man baby. Are you saying I'm a man-child? I'm saying you're a little baby.
jokes.punchline: Precise distinction between 'man-child' and 'little baby'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with semantic precision adding to the absurdity

jokes.text: Little baby wants a ride? We'll give little baby a ride.
jokes.punchline: Michael fully committing to the baby talk
jokes.explanation: Good commitment to the bit with escalating baby talk

jokes.text: Look at this thing you're walking around with, a big sack on your back. It's my knapsack!
jokes.punchline: The indignant defense of calling it a 'knapsack'
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag with character-appropriate word choice defense

jokes.text: You know what? Maybe while you're out, you could get it washed. It looks pretty clean, by the way. Do you mind? But it's totally clean.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking Larry to wash the car
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate contradiction and resistance despite agreeing
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry immediately arguing while complying

jokes.text: A card trick. Now you have to tell me when to stop. Stop. # Doo-doo, doo-doo # ...is your card
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous humming during the 'magic'
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing character quirk with the unnecessary sound effects

jokes.text: Well, you're not really a magician. Oh, yeah, I am. One trick makes you a magician? Did I trick you?
jokes.punchline: Stewart's circular logic about being tricked equals being a magician
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic battle with Stewart's absurd but internally consistent reasoning

jokes.text: He can tell that I'm a magician. Well, you can tell that I'm a magician. But I'm a magician, just naturally a magician. But you don't know any tricks.
jokes.punchline: The absurd concept of being a 'natural magician' without knowing tricks
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd escalation with perfect internal logic that makes no sense

jokes.text: if you were naturally a magician, I'd feel that you were naturally a magician.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming to be a natural magician
jokes.punchline: Stewart's mystical magician-sensing ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic - magicians can sense other natural magicians

jokes.text: You guys are a lot alike. Yeah, except he's not a magician.
jokes.setup: Cheryl noting similarity between Larry and Stewart
jokes.punchline: Larry's petty distinction about the magician thing
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Larry's petty commitment to winning the argument

jokes.text: You gonna wear a costume? Of course I'm wearing a costume, it's a Halloween party. I'm not gonna wear a costume!
jokes.punchline: Larry's defiant refusal to participate in obvious Halloween tradition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry contrarian behavior - refusing to follow obvious social norms

jokes.text: So, last night at about 11:30, I go to the bathroom to, uh, you know. You got the energy for that at 11:30 at night? I always have the energy, yeah.
jokes.setup: Jeff starting to share intimate details
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous response about having energy that late
jokes.explanation: Good setup for upcoming masturbation story with Larry's typical response

jokes.text: And, um, all of a sudden, she pops out. You know who pops in? You're not gonna believe this. Cheryl.
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Larry's wife entered his fantasy
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect timing and maximum cringe factor

jokes.text: You can't control who pops in. She pops in. Why didn't you pop her out? I tried popping her out. She wouldn't pop out.
jokes.setup: Larry's horror at his wife being in Jeff's fantasy
jokes.punchline: The absurd discussion of 'popping' people in and out of fantasies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation treating mental imagery like a controllable process

jokes.text: Besides, my bench is thin. I don't have a lot going on there. Your bench is thin? What, is she on your team now?
jokes.punchline: Larry treating the fantasy like a sports team roster
jokes.explanation: Outstanding sports metaphor for fantasy life - 'thin bench' and team membership are brilliantly absurd

jokes.text: I'll never intentionally use your wife for that. What do you mean? You can't control who pops in.
jokes.setup: Larry demanding Jeff stop using his wife in fantasies
jokes.punchline: Jeff's distinction between intentional and unintentional fantasy usage
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with the distinction between intentional vs unintentional fantasy use

jokes.text: Wait till you get the kid. It'll change your life. 'Get the kid'?
jokes.punchline: His unfortunate phrasing making adoption sound transactional
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry verbal misstep that reveals his awkward relationship with sentiment

jokes.text: Ben Stiller is gone. He is history. He came to me and said, 'It's either me or Larry David. Make up your mind.' Larry David, Stiller out.
jokes.setup: Learning about conflict with Ben Stiller
jokes.punchline: Mel's immediate choice of Larry over major star
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Mel's loyalty and Larry's obliviousness to being difficult

jokes.text: You wouldn't sit in the front of a car? You couldn't move into the front of the car?
jokes.setup: Listing Larry's offenses against Ben Stiller
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of this being a major complaint
jokes.explanation: Great callback that elevates the earlier car argument to professional grievance level

jokes.text: I happen to have a small phobia about shaking people's hands who have snot on it. Can you blame him?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he wouldn't shake Stiller's hand
jokes.punchline: The specific gross detail and Mel's immediate understanding
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's germaphobia and Mel's support

jokes.text: I don't like the 'Happy Birthday' song. He never sings the 'Happy Birthday' song. I don't like it either. I hate the song! It's a trite, cliché song.
jokes.setup: Larry's refusal to sing Happy Birthday
jokes.punchline: The escalating hatred for this innocent song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry contrarian take on universal tradition with Mel's agreement adding support

jokes.text: Blessing in disguise. He's a wonderful actor. He's a fantastic actor. I don't think he belongs in musical comedy, frankly.
jokes.punchline: Larry's backhanded dismissal disguised as professional assessment
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Larry's ability to rationalize any situation to his advantage

jokes.text: And we'd save a little money on the marquee. 'Larry David Schwimmer,' get it? Okay, bad joke.
jokes.punchline: The name combination joke and immediate self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Decent pun with self-aware acknowledgment of its weakness

jokes.text: There is something about this middle-aged bald guy that is thrilling!
jokes.punchline: Mel's enthusiastic defense using the least flattering description
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic defense that's simultaneously supportive and insulting

jokes.text: Everybody's seeing I'm putting it in my pocket, okay. Nothing's coming off this.
jokes.punchline: Blatant declaration of stealing while claiming transparency
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with the brazen announcement of theft as transparency

jokes.text: You're making it a bigger mess than it was.
jokes.setup: Car wash worker cleaning the car
jokes.punchline: Larry's complaint about professional car washing
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry complaint behavior, character-appropriate but not exceptional

jokes.text: I know this is gonna sound a little crazy, but my car ran out of gas, and I need to use a bathroom. No!
jokes.setup: Larry's polite approach to stranger's door
jokes.punchline: Immediate rejection of reasonable request
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for escalating desperation sequence

jokes.text: I'm not really a stranger. I did the 'Seinfeld' show, I've done...
jokes.setup: Trying to convince homeowner he's not a stranger
jokes.punchline: Using his TV credentials as character reference
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's celebrity entitlement in desperate situation

jokes.text: I'd like to make an offer on your house. 10 bucks for a pee.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combination of real estate and bathroom rental
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect desperation logic and quotable phrasing

jokes.text: I have a refinery in the back. I'm joking!
jokes.punchline: Haboos's deadpan oil refinery joke
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke playing on stereotypes with self-aware humor

jokes.text: We're a very superficial people. We like to know what women look like, so it's gonna be tough for you when the... I think I've got a guy for you. A blind date? Literally.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining dating difficulties while she's in burka
jokes.punchline: The literal 'blind date' pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that ties the whole episode concept together with perfect execution

jokes.text: Did you happen to see any sunblock on the passenger seat in my car? I don't know. I didn't see any.
jokes.punchline: Obvious lies from the car wash workers
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for theft revelation, not particularly sophisticated

jokes.text: weren't there four of you? Uh, no, not recently. No, at the car wash, there were four of you. Yeah, he must have gone home.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to figure out who took his sunblock
jokes.punchline: The workers' increasingly obvious lies
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the deception but not exceptional comedy

jokes.text: I think we know what happened to the sunblock. We lied! Yeah, we lied to you!
jokes.punchline: Workers' sudden enthusiastic confession
jokes.explanation: Good payoff with unexpected enthusiastic admission of guilt

jokes.text: An Arab, a Jew, and an Asian fellow... walk into a bar, okay? I didn't tell the joke yet! Wait till I tell the joke.
jokes.setup: Larry starting a potentially offensive joke
jokes.punchline: Others' preemptive negative reaction before hearing the punchline
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about racial humor expectations and prejudgment

jokes.text: Nobody knows what she looks like okay? Nobody. Everybody's in the same boat as you. You have leveled the playing field for me, Larry.
jokes.setup: Explaining the benefit of dating someone in a burka
jokes.punchline: The 'leveled playing field' concept for blind dating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about equalizing visual advantage in dating through religious clothing

jokes.text: Why do you do it so fast? Well, actually you were too fast. Oh, I was too fast? Maybe I'm just not stopping where you want me to stop.
jokes.punchline: Larry figuring out the trick and Stewart's defensive response
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier magic trick with Larry's persistence paying off

jokes.text: I'm taking half that candy. Don't say a word to her. You put it in the pantry, I'll divide it up. Understand?
jokes.punchline: Larry's plan to steal candy from a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry pettiness - stealing Halloween candy from his houseguest

jokes.text: What are you wearing? What is this, no costume? I want you to change your costume. I don't like that costume.
jokes.punchline: Susie's demand that he change his non-costume
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's costume refusal with Susie's typical demanding response

jokes.text: Haboos's burka. Just back from the drycleaners. You're going as an Islamic fundamentalist.
jokes.setup: Need for Larry to wear a costume
jokes.punchline: Larry wearing a burka as a Halloween costume
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual payoff and callback with appropriately transgressive Halloween costume choice

jokes.text: What the fuck?! Hey, Osama, go back to your own fucking country.
jokes.setup: Larry in burka walking down street
jokes.punchline: Immediate racist harassment
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing consequences of the costume choice

jokes.text: Oh! My veil!
jokes.punchline: Haboos's veil being blown off, exposing her face
jokes.explanation: Strong physical comedy payoff that resolves the central premise of the blind date

jokes.text: Not so hot, huh? No, not so hot.
jokes.setup: Seeing Haboos's face after veil removal
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact assessment of her unattractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect cruel understatement that completes the blind date disaster

jokes.text: You peeked. You bent it so you could peek at it. You looked at it from behind. How'd you know? Anyone can figure that out.
jokes.punchline: Larry's detailed explanation of the obvious trick
jokes.explanation: Good resolution to the card trick subplot with Larry's superior observation

jokes.text: And now for the rest of your life, you sick four-eyed pervert fuck, you're gonna be fantasizing about me in this outfit.
jokes.punchline: Her cruel psychological punishment through forced mental imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback culmination - Cheryl weaponizing the fantasy concept for psychological warfare. Perfect dark comedy.
